combined_verdict: REFUTED across all tested persistent-regime pairs -- period-level config-selection does NOT transfer even when the regime persists. The prior null was a REAL LIMIT, not just the crash confound. High-value honest result.

2_sub_period.CAVEAT: weekly sub-windows OVERLAP MA warmup + are autocorrelated -> supporting evidence only, NOT independent samples

3_block_bootstrap.note: resample month-1 per-config trade returns in blocks -> rank by median/p25

4_synthetic.note: score each config across synthetic Feb-like paths -> prefer robust

5_shrinkage.note: B near 0 => heavy shrink (config differences are noise vs the bootstrap noise floor); B near 1 => differences clear the noise = signal

6_regime_to_config.honest_note: map COLLAPSES to ONE config for all buckets -> the edge (if any) is a SINGLE-AXIS dominance (e.g. slow-MA-trades-rarely-survives-cost), NOT learned regime navigation. Report as such.

config_rank_persistence.interpretation: rho~+1: config ranking PERSISTS month1->month2 (regime->config transfers); rho~0: last month's config ranking is uninformative for next month (thesis FAILS even in the fair case); rho<0: anti-persistent (winner becomes loser)

verdict.headline: REFUTED (FAIR CASE): even with the regime PERSISTING month1->month2, the map COLLAPSES to one config on a majority (1/4 differentiate) -- the cost-avoidance signature, NOT learned regime navigation; it stays FAR from the oracle (>50% capture on only 0/4) -- beating random is not the same as approaching the hindsight best. So the prior NULL was NOT merely the crash confound -- period-level config-selection does NOT transfer even in the fair case. The honest result: 'config-selection doesn't transfer even when the regime persists.' Reported as a refutation, not spun.
lines: Regime persistence MEASURED: 9/10 assets keep the same regime month1->month2 (0.9); train mix {'down': 9, 'flat': 1} -> test mix {'down': 10}.

HEADLINE: REFUTED (FAIR CASE): even with the regime PERSISTING month1->month2, the map COLLAPSES to one config on a majority (1/4 differentiate) -- the cost-avoidance signature, NOT learned regime navigation; it stays FAR from the oracle (>50% capture on only 0/4) -- beating random is not the same as approaching the hindsight best. So the prior NULL was NOT merely the crash confound -- period-level config-selection does NOT transfer even in the fair case. The honest result: 'config-selection doesn't transfer even when the regime persists.' Reported as a refutation, not spun.
[15m] config-rank rho(book)=0.889 | map-differentiates=False (1 cfgs) | pred 0.24% / oracle 7.9% (ratio 0.03) | book BEATS random
[30m] config-rank rho(book)=0.895 | map-differentiates=False (1 cfgs) | pred -0.06% / oracle 7.65% (ratio -0.01) | book BEATS random
[1h] config-rank rho(book)=0.909 | map-differentiates=True (2 cfgs) | pred 2.97% / oracle 8.07% (ratio 0.37) | book BEATS random
[4h] config-rank rho(book)=0.762 | map-differentiates=False (1 cfgs) | pred 1.6% / oracle 6.48% (ratio 0.25) | book BEATS random
Q4 config-rank persists (book rho>+0.30) on 4/4 (majority=True); mean book rho=0.864.
